About me
Strategy meets
systems thinking.

I'm a Data & Systems Analyst who thrives at the intersection of data, automation, and decision-making. At Unique Biotech Limited, I've built revenue systems from scratch — expo intelligence, CRM automation, outreach funnels, and board-level dashboards.

I don't just analyze data. I build the infrastructure that makes decisions faster, pipelines cleaner, and growth more predictable. B.Tech in Computer Science from GGSIPU with a 9.0 GPA.

Data Analyst & System Designer

Mental Models
How I think.
Systems Thinking
Full lifecycle design — Pre → During → Post, nothing left to chance
First-Principles
Rebuilt logic from base metrics, not templates or assumptions
Theory of Constraints
Find bottlenecks — reply delays, no-shows, drop-offs — then remove them
AIDCA Framework
Structured outreach architecture driving engagement and conversion
Lean Startup
Build MVPs → iterate fast → scale what works
Life-Cycle Thinking
Every lead mapped: TOFU → MOFU → BOFU with logic at each stage

🎯
My full vision — how I help organizations grow
Revenue · Operations · Founder partnership · Team transformation
The core belief

Most organizations don't fail because of a bad idea. They fail because the gap between vision and execution never gets bridged. I exist to bridge that gap — through data, systems, and people who own outcomes instead of just completing tasks.

I want to be the person who makes the whole company level up — not just one function, not just one metric, but the entire operating rhythm of how a business thinks, moves, and grows.

The four pillars of my vision
📈
Revenue growth — making the number move

Revenue is the oxygen of any organization. My role is to build the systems, pipelines, and intelligence that turn strategy into actual money — not projected money, not hoped-for money. I do this by identifying exactly where the opportunity is, building the outreach and conversion infrastructure around it, and creating dashboards that make progress visible in real time so leadership can make faster, smarter bets.

⚙
Operational excellence — removing what slows you down

The biggest invisible cost in most companies is friction — the manual work, the repeated errors, the meetings that replace systems, the data that lives in someone's head instead of a dashboard. I find those friction points and I eliminate them. Not by adding more tools, but by designing clean workflows that reduce human dependency, scale without breaking, and give everyone clarity on what's happening and what needs to happen next.

💡
Founder partnership — from imagination to execution

Founders see things others can't. The problem is translating that vision into something the organization can actually execute on. I work directly at the founder level — taking their ideas, their instincts, their long-term ambition, and turning it into structured systems, clear data, and actionable strategy. I become the bridge between what a founder imagines and what the company actually builds. This is not just execution — it's a thinking partnership.

💪
Team mindset — from task completion to growth ownership

The most powerful thing you can do for an organization is change how its people think. I've seen teams that complete every task but never move the needle — because they're executing without ownership. My vision is to shift that: to create environments where people don't just do their job but feel responsible for the company's growth. Where accountability isn't top-down pressure but internal drive. Where "how do we grow?" becomes everyone's question, not just the founder's.

What this looks like in practice
  • Walking into an organization and mapping the full revenue and operational system within the first two weeks
  • Finding the one bottleneck that's costing the most and removing it before anything else
  • Sitting with the founder weekly to translate strategy into data and data into decisions
  • Building dashboards that make every person in the company feel connected to the outcome
  • Creating SOPs and systems that don't depend on any one person to function
  • Mentoring team members to stop asking "what do I do?" and start asking "what should we do?"
  • Measuring success not just in revenue but in how much faster the organization makes good decisions
The long-term goal

I want to build a track record of organizations that grew faster, operated cleaner, and thought bigger because I was in the room. Not as a consultant who delivered a report and left — but as someone who was embedded in the mission, who cared about the outcome as much as the founder, and who built something that lasted long after the engagement ended.

That's the kind of impact I'm building toward.
